zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Randolph Countess of Dunbar and March於大約1300年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾鄧巴時，她的父親Thomas Randolph 1st Earl of Moray16歲，母親Isabella Stewart14歲。她和Sir Patrick Dunbar of Swinwood至少有6個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於大約1369年在她的家鄉享年71歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭貝維其可爾莫丁頓 麥斯。

nameMeanings.name: Randolph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rand
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Rand, a pet form of Randolf. Compare Randolph , Randall , Randy , and see also Rance .
English: habitational name from Rand (Lincolnshire), Rand Grange (North Yorkshire), or Raunds (Northamptonshire), all of which derive from Old English rand ‘edge, border, rim’.
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic compound personal names formed with rand ‘rim (of a shield)’. Compare 1 above.

variants.name: Randerson
meanings: English (West and East Yorkshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Randolf (see Randolph ) + -son. In East Anglia the name may also have been influenced by a form of the now rare or extinct Rendlesham, itself from the place of that name in Suffolk. The placename probably derives from an Old English personal name Rendel (genitive Rendeles) + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’.

variants.name: Randolf
meanings: English and German: variant of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randall
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Randolph Countess of Dunbar and March was born about 1300, in Dunbar, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, her father, Thomas Randolph 1st Earl of Moray, was 16 and her mother, Isabella Stewart, was 14. She had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters with Sir Patrick Dunbar of Swinwood. She died about 1369, in her hometown, at the age of 71, and was buried in Mordington Mains, Berwickshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Randolph Countess of Dunbar and March est née environ 1300, à Dunbar, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, Thomas Randolph 1st Earl of Moray, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Isabella Stewart, avait 14 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles avec Sir Patrick Dunbar of Swinwood. Elle est décédée environ 1369, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Mordington Mains, Berwickshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Randolph Countess of Dunbar and March um 1300 in Dunbar, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Randolph 1st Earl of Moray 16 und ihre Mutter Isabella Stewart 14. Sie hatte mit Sir Patrick Dunbar of Swinwood mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1369 im Alter von 71 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Mordington Mains, Berwickshire, Scotland begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Randolph Countess of Dunbar and March(이)가 대략 1300년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 던바에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Randolph 1st Earl of Moray(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Stewart(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 Sir Patrick Dunbar of Swinwood(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1369년 그녀의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 버윅실르 모르딩턴 맹스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Randolph Countess of Dunbar and March родилась около 1300 г. в местности дунбар, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Thomas Randolph 1st Earl of Moray было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Stewart, 14.
